65d14219270be3aedf05a344.question: Letters that this author wrote to Ellen Nussey were the primary source for the first biography of this author. The Lucy poems by Wordsworth and the teacher Constantin Héger inspired major characters in a novel by this author, according to the scholars Gilbert and Gubar. This author was the primary creator of the fictional setting of Glass Town. The first biography of this novelist was written by Elizabeth Gaskell. This author inspired a later writer to create the character Antoinette (*) Cosway. A novel by this author inspired the title of the 1979 book The Madwoman in the Attic, as well as the Jean Rhys novel Wide Sargasso Sea. This author created Bertha Mason, the first wife of Mr Rochester. For 10 points, name this author of Jane Eyre.

65d14219270be3aedf05a34a.question: In an essay by John Darnielle, this composer inspires the singer to think of “U.G. Krishnamurti…Current 93…and endless smoke”. After a dialogue with Toru Takemitsu, this composer decided to translate a Basho haiku as “What mushroom? / What leaf?” With the violinist Paul Zukofsky, this man composed the Freeman Etudes. The St. Burchadi Church in Halberstadt is the site of this composer’s very long organ piece As Slow as Possible. Performances of a 1951 indeterminate composition by this composer are guided by the (*) I Ching. David Tudor premiered this composer’s piece Music of Changes. This composer of the Imaginary Landscapes series made early, and regular, use of the prepared piano. For 10 points, name this minimalist composer of 4’33”.
65d14219270be3aedf05a34a.question_sanitized: In an essay by John Darnielle, this composer inspires the singer to think of "U.G. Krishnamurti...Current 93...and endless smoke". After a dialogue with Toru Takemitsu, this composer decided to translate a Basho haiku as "What mushroom? / What leaf?" With the violinist Paul Zukofsky, this man composed the Freeman Etudes. The St. Burchadi Church in Halberstadt is the site of this composer's very long organ piece As Slow as Possible. Performances of a 1951 indeterminate composition by this composer are guided by the (*) I Ching. David Tudor premiered this composer's piece Music of Changes. This composer of the Imaginary Landscapes series made early, and regular, use of the prepared piano. For 10 points, name this minimalist composer of 4'33".
65d14219270be3aedf05a34a.answer_sanitized: John Cage [or John Milton Cage Jr.]

65d1421a270be3aedf05a35e.leadin: This artist requested that his paintings of the prisoner abuse at Abu Ghraib should be exhibited at all times as a “permanent accusation.” For 10 points each:
65d1421a270be3aedf05a35e.leadin_sanitized: This artist requested that his paintings of the prisoner abuse at Abu Ghraib should be exhibited at all times as a "permanent accusation." For 10 points each:

Botero is known for his fat, baby-like parodies of historical portraits, including this one by Leonardo da Vinci. The woman in this portrait has an enigmatic smile and seems to lack eyebrows.
Botero has also parodied this artist’s portrait of Pope Leo X (“the tenth”), in which he is depicted between two cardinals. This artist’s paintings decorate the Stanza della segnatura.

Botero has also parodied this artist's portrait of Pope Leo X ("the tenth"), in which he is depicted between two cardinals. This artist's paintings decorate the Stanza della segnatura.
answers: the Mona Lisa [or la Gioconda or la Joconde; accept Mona Lisa: Age Twelve]
answers_sanitized: the Mona Lisa [or la Gioconda or la Joconde; accept Mona Lisa: Age Twelve]

65d1421b270be3aedf05a3c3.question: In an ekphrasis (“ECK-fra-sis”) on a painting by this artist, Denis Diderot (“denny DEE-de-roh”) recalls a dream of Plato's cave that conveniently evokes details of this painter's Coresus Sacrificing Himself to Save Callirhoe (“kuh-LIR-oh-ee”). The reception of that painting led this artist to shun the popular mode of history painting in favor of romantic commissions, such as a painting by this artist of a man attempting to lock the door while kissing his lover. This artist of The (*) Bolt included an Etienne Falconet (“fal-co-NAY”) statue of a cupid holding a finger to his lips in a painting set in a flowery grove. A man stares up the skirt of a woman who is kicking her shoe off while aboard the title object in a painting by this artist. For 10 points, name this French Rococo painter of The Swing.

65d1421b270be3aedf05a3d2.leadin: This artist writes captions into his paintings that he calls a way of “not allowing freedom of interpretation.” For 10 points each:
65d1421b270be3aedf05a3d2.leadin_sanitized: This artist writes captions into his paintings that he calls a way of "not allowing freedom of interpretation." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist whose self-portrait, The Draughtsman (“draftsman”), includes the caption “caricatural drawing is nothing because anyone can do it” in English, French, and Lingala.

Chéri Samba’s paintings often have multiple panels in addition to captions, thanks to his background in this medium. During the pop art movement, Roy Lichtenstein used ben-day dots to imitate this medium.
Chéri Samba’s painting J’aime la couleur (“JEM la coo-LUR”) is a surreal self-portrait parodying this artist’s works, most notably the woodcut Rind and the lithograph Bond of Union.

65d1421c270be3aedf05a43e.question: At the premiere of a work by this composer, critic Roger Nichols disparaged the “horrible screeching from solo violin” and the “deformed Dies Irae,” (“DEE-ess EE-ray”) which is unusually in a major key. An arrangement for cello with piano accompaniment is the only part of this composer’s most famous work that he allowed to be played publicly before his death. In a famous use of scordatura, this composer had the E string of the violin tuned down to E-flat to create a tritone with A, which is played following (*) twelve D notes on harp. Zygmunt Stojowski (“ZIG-munt sto-YOV-ski”) joked that this composer’s second piano concerto “begins with Bach and ends with Offenbach.” This composer imitated his own Danse macabre (“DONS ma-COB”) by using xylophones to imitate clattering bones in a movement that quotes Twinkle Twinkle Little Star. For 10 points, name this composer of Swan and Fossils, part of his Carnival of the Animals.

65d1421c270be3aedf05a460.question: The musician Reginal Smith Brindle composed settings of the poems of Garcia Lorca for this instrument. J.S. Bach composed an E minor piece titled Bourrée for this instrument. It’s not a violin, but Lee Krasner owned the extremely-rare Rawlins one of these instruments produced by Antonio Stradivari. A strict teacher of this instrument criticised the unorthodox style that John Williams used when playing this instrument with the group Sky. The iconic (*) Nokia theme was taken from Gran Vals, a composition for this instrument by Francisco Tárrega. Joaquín (“wa-KEEN”) Rodrigo composed the Concierto de Aranjuez (“con-see-AIR-to day a-RAHN-ways”) for this instrument. Andrés Segovia and Julian Bream were virtuosos of this instrument. For 10 points, name this instrument which typically has six strings and is related to the lute.

Topp used a sciopticon (“sigh-OP-ti-con”) to show the earliest form of these things which he patented in 1909. The website Niconico (“neeko-neeko”) pioneered a form of these things known as danmaku, named for resembling a bullet curtain. 65d1421c270be3aedf05a468.question: John Martin Poyer was awarded the Navy Cross for actions he took during this year as Governor of American Samoa. Henry Gunther died in this year, and the sailor-led Kiel Mutiny occurred in this year. The first notable usage of the Wagon of Compiègne (“kom-PYEN”) occurred during this year. Both Egon Schiele (“sheila”) and Gustav Klimt (*) died in this year. A massive Liberty Loans parade in Philadelphia during this year has been called the deadliest parade in history, with 12,000 deaths among attendees.
